meaning: v.(使)旋转,急动,急走n.旋转,—连串快速的活动
examples: To free electrons, something has to make them whirl fast enough to break away from their nuclei.
I learn not to separate these creatures bursting with violent energy from the sky where their desires whirl.
Planets are believed to emerge from the accretion of particles in a disk of gas and dust as they whirl around the star.
If the Magic Mouse is a piece of flat land, the Mini Whirl is, well, Everest.
"They're getting birdy, " he says, as the dogs' tails begin to whirl in tight circles.
